step_rationale: Direct marker of thrombin generation at injury site; anchor for coagulation activation.
mechanism: TAT complexes quantify active thrombin generation at the site of coronary injury, marking the intensity of coagulation cascade activation in acute MI.

references.title: Microparticle-linked tissue factor activity and increased thrombin activity play a potential role in fibrinolysis failure in ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction.
Thrombin formation and platelet activation at the site of vascular injury in patients with coronary artery disease treated with clopidogrel combined with aspirin.
Absence of paradoxical thrombin activation by fibrin-specific thrombolytics in acute myocardial infarction: comparison of single-bolus tenecteplase and front-loaded alteplase.
